title: eriostemon australasius
intro: Eriostemon australasius, also known as the New South Wales fruited paperbark, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to eastern Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Eriostemon australasius has been used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, skin irritations, and wounds, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support the body's natural healing processes and provide relief from minor health complaints.
